condition: Medication does not directly produce drug_exposure rows. Its code/ingredient data flows into drug_exposure via event resources (MedicationRequest, MedicationDispense, MedicationAdministration, MedicationStatement) when they carry a medicationReference pointing to a Medication resource.
narrative_md: The FHIR Medication resource is a vocabulary/codeable-concept resource, not a medication event. It does NOT directly produce a drug_exposure row. Instead, its code and ingredient data are resolved into drug_concept_id and drug_source_value on drug_exposure rows produced by the four event resources (MedicationRequest, MedicationDispense, MedicationAdministration, MedicationStatement). This edge documents how Medication fields contribute to drug_exposure columns when referenced.

fields.transform: Vocabulary lookup: resolve best coding (RxNorm > NDC > ATC > SNOMED) to OMOP standard concept via CONCEPT table
fields.notes: Set by the event mapper after resolving medicationReference. The 'best' coding is selected by vocabulary priority.
Raw source code from the highest-priority coding in Medication.code

fields.transform: Look up source vocabulary concept_id for the raw code (non-standard concept)
fields.notes: The source concept before mapping to standard vocabulary

entries.notes: US primary drug vocabulary. Preferred source for drug_concept_id. Maps directly to OMOP standard concepts.
US National Drug Code. Requires mapping to RxNorm standard concept via CONCEPT_RELATIONSHIP.
European classification. Used by ETL-German for German MII data. Extracted from Medication.ingredient[].itemCodeableConcept.
SNOMED CT product codes. Requires mapping to RxNorm standard concept.

references.notes: Resolves contained #-references in event resources. Iterates contained[] to find Medication by ID fragment, extracts Medication.code.
Same contained-reference resolution logic as MedicationRequest mapper.
Two-phase merge: produces partial drug_exposure rows with concept info only; merged by ID with rows from MedicationRequest.jq in post-processing.
Pre-indexed map. Walks all Medication resources up front, builds medication_id_map keyed by resource ID for reference resolution. Extracts ATC codes from ingredients.
Maps MedicationStatement only and assumes inline coding. Does not address Medication resource resolution.

edge_cases.handling: Event resources may carry either medicationReference (pointing to a Medication resource) or inline medicationCodeableConcept. When only medicationReference is present, the Medication resource must be resolved to extract the code. Several implementations skip medicationReference entirely and only process inline codes.

Medication is embedded in the event resource's contained[] array. Iterate contained resources to find a Medication matching the fragment ID. Extract Medication.code. Fall back to Reference.display if no code found.

Medication resources appear as separate entries in a Bundle alongside event resources. Requires pre-indexing all Medications by fullUrl/id before processing events. Two strategies: build an in-memory map (ETL-German) or produce partial rows and merge in post-processing (fhir-to-omop-demo).
Some Medication resources carry no top-level code but have ingredient[].itemCodeableConcept with ATC codes. ETL-German extracts ATC from ingredients as the drug source value.
Pick best coding by vocabulary priority: RxNorm > NDC > ATC > SNOMED > first. Use the selected code for drug_source_value and vocab lookup for drug_concept_id.
Not directly mapped to any OMOP column. Dose form is captured implicitly via drug_concept_id when using RxNorm clinical/branded drug products that encode form.
Dropped. No corresponding OMOP drug_exposure columns.
